topic: Quantum computing applications in cryptography post-2022
final_review:
The advent of quantum computing has significant implications for cryptography, as quantum computers can potentially break certain classical encryption algorithms (Lenstra et al., 2001). In response, researchers have been exploring post-quantum cryptography, which refers to cryptographic techniques that are resistant to quantum computing attacks (Fernández‐Caramés et al., 2020). One area of focus is the development of new cryptographic algorithms and protocols that can withstand quantum computing attacks. 

In recent years, there has been a growing interest in lattice-based post-quantum cryptography algorithms, which have been shown to be efficient and secure (Dang et al., 2019). These algorithms have been implemented using software/hardware codesign, which allows for optimized performance (Dang et al., 2019). Additionally, researchers have been exploring the use of post-quantum cryptography in blockchain, which is a distributed ledger technology that relies on cryptographic techniques (Fernández‐Caramés et al., 2020). The use of post-quantum cryptography in blockchain can provide enhanced security against quantum computing attacks (Fernández‐Caramés et al., 2020).

The development of post-quantum cryptography is an active area of research, with several organizations, including the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), working on standardizing post-quantum cryptography algorithms (Alagic et al., 2022). The NIST post-quantum cryptography standardization process has been ongoing since 2016, and several algorithms have been selected for further evaluation (Alagic et al., 2022). Furthermore, researchers have been exploring the use of post-quantum cryptography in secure boot protocols, which are used to ensure the secure booting of devices (Kumar et al., 2020).

In the context of 6G wireless communication systems, post-quantum cryptography will play a crucial role in ensuring the security of these systems (Jiang et al., 2021; de Alwis et al., 2021). The use of post-quantum cryptography in 6G will require the development of new algorithms and protocols that can meet the high-speed and low-latency requirements of these systems (Wang et al., 2023). Moreover, the integration of post-quantum cryptography with other technologies, such as artificial intelligence, will be essential for ensuring the security of 6G systems (Hassija et al., 2023).

In conclusion, post-quantum cryptography is a rapidly evolving field, with significant implications for the security of cryptographic systems (Gisin et al., 2002). The development of post-quantum cryptography algorithms and protocols is an active area of research, with several organizations and researchers working on standardizing and implementing these techniques (Preskill, 2018). Further research is needed to address the challenges and opportunities in this field, including the development of new algorithms and protocols, and the integration of post-quantum cryptography with other technologies (Farooq et al., 2023).

References:
Alagic, G., Apon, D., & Cooper, D. A. (2022). Status report on the third round of the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization process. https://doi.org/10.6028/nist.ir.8413
Dang, V. B., Farahmand, F., & Andrzejczak, M. (2019). Implementing and Benchmarking Three Lattice-Based Post-Quantum Cryptography Algorithms Using Software/Hardware Codesign. https://doi.org/10.1109/icfpt47387.2019.00032
de Alwis, C., Kalla, A., & Pham, Q. V. (2021). Survey on 6G Frontiers: Trends, Applications, Requirements, Technologies and Future Research. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3071496
Farooq, S., Altaf, A., & Iqbal, F. (2023). Resilience Optimization of Post-Quantum Cryptography Key Encapsulation Algorithms. https://doi.org/10.3390/s23125379
Fernández‐Caramés, T. M., & Fraga‐Lamas, P. (2020). Towards Post-Quantum Blockchain: A Review on Blockchain Cryptography Resistant to Quantum Computing Attacks. https://doi.org/10.1109/access.2020.2968985
Gisin, N., Ribordy, G., & Tittel, W. (2002). Quantum cryptography. https://doi.org/10.1103/revmodphys.74.145
Hassija, V., Chamola, V., & Mahapatra, A. (2023). Interpreting Black-Box Models: A Review on Explainable Artificial Intelligence. https://doi.org/10.1007/s12559-023-10179-8
Jiang, W., Han, B., & Habibi, M. A. (2021). The Road Towards 6G: A Comprehensive Survey. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3057679
Kumar, V., Gupta, N., & Chattopadhyay, A. (2020). Post-Quantum Secure Boot. https://doi.org/10.23919/date48585.2020.9116252
Lenstra, A. K., & Verheul, E. R. (2001). Selecting Cryptographic Key Sizes. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00145-001-0009-4
Preskill, J. (2018). Quantum Computing in the NISQ era and beyond. https://doi.org/10.22331/q-2018-08-06-79
